title: kube play does not follow Kubernetes precedence order for env and envFrom
body:
### Issue Description

In Kubernetes, if the same environment variable is defined in both the `envFrom` and `env` sections, the version from `env` takes precedence. With `podman kube play`, the version from the `envFrom` section takes precedence.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create `test.yaml` as follows:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: test-pod
spec:
  containers:
    - name: test-container
      image: busybox
      command: ["/bin/sh", "-ec", "sleep infinity"]
      envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: test-config-map
      env:
        - name: MY_ENV_VAR
          value: "2"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: test-config-map
data:
  MY_ENV_VAR: "1"

```
2. Run the pod: `podman kube play test.yaml`
3. Check the value of the environment variable: `podman exec test-pod-test-container /bin/sh -c 'echo $MY_ENV_VAR'`


### Describe the results you received

The value of `MY_ENV_VAR` is `1`. This is the value from the `envFrom` section.

### Describe the results you expected

The expected value of `MY_ENV_VAR` is `2`. This is the value from the `env` section.

comments.body: I'm not very familiar with Golang...but sure, I can give it a try! It looks to me like these two blocks just need to be reversed so that `envFrom` is processed first: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/32d6c540542df8bf2115a8600fd4fbbf4b6b837a/pkg/specgen/generate/kube/kube.go#L474-L492
Yeah, I think that ought to work fine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I prepared a PR here, as it looks unattended to
